title: Velvet Kale Delight
description: A velvety and bitter kale dish infused with the essence of tadka (a blend of spices), perfect for a romantic dinner or date night in Chinese cuisine.

ingredients.name: 1/2 tsp cumin seeds
ingredients.amount: lightly roasted for depth of flavor

ingredients.name: 1 tbsp tamari sauce or soy sauce

instructions: Heat a wok over medium-high heat. Add the sesame oil, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, and black pepper powder. Toast until fragrant and slightly crispy.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the hot pan. Sauté for about 1 minute, stirring occasionally.
Increase heat to high and add the cashews. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes or until they start to brown lightly.
Fold in the kale leaves by bunches into the wok, ensuring all leaves are evenly coated with the tadka mixture.
Reduce heat to medium-high and cook for another 5-7 minutes, tossing regularly so no sticking occurs. Kale should be soft but still retain a slight bite.
Stir in tamari sauce or soy sauce last, then stir well to combine. Remove from heat immediately to prevent further cooking which can make kale lose its crispness.
Serve hot as a side dish or use it as the base for other dishes like a wrap or sandwich filling.
